name: Hawaii hotel cleaning chemicals made me dizzy and nauseous—what are my legal rights?
description: You are entitled to medical coverage and wage replacement. Because cleaning chemicals often cause 'invisible' respiratory or neurological injuries, documenting

mainEntity.name: Hawaii hotel cleaning chemicals made me dizzy and nauseous—what are my legal rights?

acceptedAnswer.text: You are entitled to medical coverage and wage replacement. Because cleaning chemicals often cause 'invisible' respiratory or neurological injuries, documenting the specific products and reporting symptoms immediately is vital to proving your case.
acceptedAnswer.description:
Cleaning chemicals are a major cause of "occupational asthma" and neurological issues. As an employee in Hawaii, you have the right to medical treatment and temporary disability pay if you cannot work. The challenge is often documenting the specific brand and concentration of the chemicals used, as many hotel products are bought in bulk and diluted, making their exact ingredients hard to trace later.

Steps for cleaning-related claims:
- Report the dizziness immediately; these are acute symptoms that prove exposure.
- Take a photo of the label on the chemical bottles you use.
- Ask to see the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) for those products.

If the dizziness caused a fall or secondary injury, those are covered too. Do not let the employer tell you that you are "just sensitive" to the smell. If it's making you sick, it's a workplace injury. A lawyer can help ensure the hotel provides the full chemical profile and doesn't hide "proprietary" ingredient lists.
